titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD45/13/262

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=248 status=live modified=2018-07-31 01:59:31.512532+00 | translation moa=249 status=live modified=2017-02-17 01:44:37.072284+00

div.p:
Uniuersis Christi fidelibus Presentem Cartam Visuris uel Audituris . Rogerus de Qvenc’ Comes Wyntonie Constabularius Scocie
Salutem. Vniuersitati uestre notum facio me dedisse Concessisse . ⁊ presenti Carta mea confirmasse Pro salute anime mee
⁊ predecessorum meorum Abbati ⁊ conuentus De sancta Cruce de Edenburg’ . ⁊ tenentibus eorum in prestona quod quieti sint In
perpetuum de sequela molendini mei de Tauernent . Et de multura omnimodi Bladi tocius terre de prestuna ad ecclesiam
suam de Trauernent spectantis. Qvod  ut perpetue firmitatis robur optineat  presentem cartam sigilli mei
munimine Roborandam Dignumduxi. Datum apud Trauernent . die Lune proxima ante festum apostolorum
Symonis ⁊ Iude . Anno Gracie . Millesimo . Ducentesimo . Quinquagesimo.

To the whole body of Christ’s faithful who shall view or hear the present charter, Roger de Quincy, earl of Winchester, constable of Scotland, sends Greeting: I make known to you all that I have given, granted, and by my present charter made firm, for the salvation of my own soul and my predecessors’, to the abbot and convent of Holyrood in Edinburgh, and to their tenants in Preston, that they be quit in perpetuity of suit of my mill of Tranent, and of multure of all manner of grain of the whole land of Preston pertaining to their church of Tranent. That this may obtain the strength of endurance, I have duly caused the present charter to be strengthened with the defence of my seal. Given at Tranent, on the Monday next before the feast of the apostles Simon and Jude, in the year of grace 1250.
